question:
Will the price of Bitcoin be above $90,000 on November 30?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

evidences.content: ## Bitcoin Predictions for 2025 Bitcoin will cross $150k in H1 and test or best $185k in Q4 2025. A combination of institutional, corporate, and nation-state adoption will propel Bitcoin to new heights in 2025. Throughout its existence, Bitcoin has appreciated faster than all other asset classes, particularly the S&P 500 and gold, and that trend will continue in 2025. Bitcoin will also reach 20% of Gold’s market cap. -Alex Thorn Note: A prior version of the graphic above misstated some CAGR numbers for Gold and S&P 500. In 2024, the Bitcoin ETPs collectively took in more than $36bn in net inflows, making them the best ETP launch as a cohort in history. Many of the world’s major hedge funds bought the Bitcoin ETPs, including Millennium, Tudor, and D.E. evidences.title: Bitcoin's Price History
evidences.content: ### Late 2024 On Nov. 7, 2024, Bitcoin's price hit another all-time high of $75,902 following Donald Trump's re-election, a 9% increase from the closing price on Nov. 5. On Nov. 10, 2024, Bitcoin reached another milestone, crossing the $80,000 threshold.1 On Nov. 11, it continued pushing boundaries, and two days later, it closed at $90,574.1 Market exuberance continued apace in the following weeks, pushing Bitcoin to remarkable highs of $99,817 on Oanda, $99,860 on Coinbase, and $99,852 on Gemini on Nov. 22, 2024, by midday.8 9 10 On Dec. 8, 2024, Bitcoin broke through $100,000 and closed at $101,237.1 [...] ## 2024 Price History In January 2024, the long fight for Bitcoin Spot ETFs came to a close after the SEC was forced by courts to review its denial of certain Bitcoin-related investment products.4 Some brokerages swarmed the market and increased their holdings, while others, like Grayscale's Bitcoin Trust (GBTC), saw significant outflows from the start.5 The outflows from certain funds slowed going into March, somewhat settling the market. The market-wide rebalancing was likely because investors had more options to choose from. ### Early 2024 Bitcoin's price climbed quickly...

Price targets in this article rely on assumptions made regarding the total addressable markets (TAMs) and penetration rates discussed below. evidences.title: Market Byte: Bitcoin Futures ETFs - A Step In The Right Direction | Grayscale
evidences.content: Source: Bloomberg (Date: 10/22/2021) Since BITO utilizes CME Bitcoin Futures contracts to simulate Bitcoin exposure, this pent-up investor demand for crypto likely contributed to the sharp rise we’ve seen in CME Bitcoin Futures open interest. As a result, the CME’s share of global crypto open interest has increased from 11% to 23% since the end of Q2 2021, with ~$1.9 billion or ~5% of this increase coming over the last week since the US Bitcoin Futures ETF launched. FIGURE 4: CME BITCOIN FUTURES OPEN INTEREST & MARKET SHARE Source: Skew (Date: 10/25/2021) [...] This differential in the price of CME Bitcoin Futures contracts above the spot price means that futures-based ETF investors may be gaining exposure by buying high and selling low, which could be a high cost implicitly built into the product, depending on futures market conditions. Following the US Bitcoin Futures ETF launch last week, capital flows into BITO helped push the CME Bitcoin Futures rolling annualized 1-month basis to 24%, before returning to 14% today. [...] Source: Skew (Date: 10/25/2021) One positive implication of the US Bitcoin Futures ETF is that this increased futures demand has sprung the CME ahead of o...

evidences.title: Bitcoin Price Phases: Navigating Bitcoin's Volatility Trends
evidences.content: The thesis that bitcoin entered the Appreciation Phase in February was further strengthened by a new all-time closing high of nearly $69,000 on March 4, 2024. With new all-time highs in price making global headlines, it became increasingly likely that low volatility would soon give way to high volatility and the Acceleration Phase. [...] On July 15, 2024, one-year realized volatility surpassed the fifth percentile, marking the beginning of the Acceleration Phase—a period of heightened volatility and profitability. In previous cycles, this phase has seen bitcoin reach new all-time highs, culminating in a blow-off top and cycle peak. This phase is not characterized by a straight upward trajectory, however. Instead, it can be marked with sharp volatility in both directions. This current cycle is shaping up similarly to both the 2013 and 2017 Acceleration Phases, which can be viewed in more detail in the appendix, “Bitcoin Price Phase Heat Maps (2010–2025)”. [...] As shown in the appendix, bitcoin’s price on June 13, 2023, was an unassuming $25,897. However—like an underlying current on seemingly calm waters—a shift in volatility was occurring. In this way, volatility plays a crucia...

evidences.title: Revisiting the Bitcoin Basis: How Momentum & Sentiment Impact the ...
evidences.content: CF BenchmarksCF benchmarks logo ## Oct 16, 2025 # Revisiting the Bitcoin Basis: How Momentum & Sentiment Impact the Structural Drivers of Basis Activity The CME Bitcoin futures basis is primarily driven by price momentum and market sentiment, which may be derived from both macroeconomic and idiosyncratic factors. Market structure dynamics allow sophisticated institutional traders to capitalize on predictable patterns where aggressive Bitcoin price expansion and bullish sentiment create a positive basis, while aggressive price declines and bearish sentiment drive a negative basis. This momentum- and sentiment-driven relationship, supported by aligned pricing benchmarks between spot ETFs and futures, has fundamentally transformed Bitcoin derivatives markets. [...] ## The Interplay of Spot Bitcoin ETFs and Futures Markets The launch of spot Bitcoin ETFs in the U.S. marked a significant shift in the futures landscape. The ETF structure has provided institutions with a regulated and liquid spot leg, making basis strategies more scalable. As shown in Figure 3, leveraged funds increased their net short positioning in CME Bitcoin futures following the ETF launch. This shift was not nece...

evidences.title: Bitcoin Price - History, Chart, 5 Years, 10 Years, Halving
evidences.content: ## What is Bitcoin's all-time high price record? November 2021 saw Bitcoin's all-time high price record, around $69,000. This milestone captures the rising institutional interest in blockchain technology as well as their acceptance. But Bitcoin's volatility means its price might vary greatly, hence investors have to exercise great caution. The price swings of Bitcoin rely on several elements, including market mood, laws, and more general state of the economy. Investing in Bitcoin presents hazards even if it presents chances. Before investing, one should investigate carefully and get financial guidance. [...] But the trip of Bitcoin has been far from consistent. Sharp variations have come from market corrections. Driven by rising institutional investment and inflation worries, Bitcoin peaked in 2021 at previously unheard-of levels—above $60,000. These benefits, meantime, come with more volatility and government scrutiny. Though still prone to swings, by 2022 Bitcoin's price had found a more consistent range. From its modest start to a worldwide financial asset, the development of cryptocurrencies and changing viewpoint on them are highlighted. The price of Bitcoin reflects larger...

evidences.title: The State of the Crypto Cycle - Grayscale Research
evidences.content: Article background Research Reports The State of the Crypto... ### The State of the Crypto Cycle Last Updated: 5/29/2024 | 14 min. read Like many physical commodities, Bitcoin’s price does not follow a strict “random walk.”(#_ftn1) Instead, prices show evidence of statistical momentum: gains tend to follow gains, and losses tend to follow losses. Over longer time frames, these recurring periods of Bitcoin appreciation and depreciation give the appearance of price cycles around a historically rising trend (Exhibit 1). Exhibit 1: Bitcoin’s price characterized by recurring cycles around rising trend [...] After peaking in November 2021, Bitcoin’s price declined to a cyclical low of about $16k in November 2022. The current period of price appreciation began at that point and has therefore lasted just over two years. As shown in Exhibit 2, the latest rise in prices has tracked relatively closely to the last two Bitcoin cycles, which both continued for about one more year before prices peaked. From a magnitude standpoint, Bitcoin’s roughly 6x return this cycle, while meaningful, is significantly less than the returns achieved during each of the past four cycles. In summary, while we c...

evidences.title: Bitcoin's Market Cycle & Crypto Cycles Chart | Caleb & Brown
evidences.content: Historically, bitcoin has followed a four-year cycle tied to Bitcoin halving events, which happen approximately every four years. A halving event marks a 50% cut in the bitcoin reward miners receive for mining new blocks and verifying transactions. In effect, the bitcoin supply continues to increase, but at a slower rate, upholding the network’s deflationary principles. The knock-on effect has led to steep price increases, driven by speculation and a decrease in the supply of new bitcoin entering circulation. The last halving occurred on April 19, 2024, when the block reward decreased from 6.25 to 3.125 bitcoins. The next halving is expected in April 2028. ## What are the Bitcoin Market Cycle Phases? [...] Sell volume builds as a portion of investors lock in healthy profits, while many market participants continue to buy, believing the bull market has more room to run. As a result, price volatility is low given that buy and sell volumes begin to balance against a backdrop of overconfidence. At this point, the Fear & Greed Index typically flashes Extreme Greed. #### Phase 4 – Crash Following the euphoria of the bubble phase, the market will experience a major correction to the do...

evidences.title: Standard Chartered Slashes 2025 Bitcoin Forecast to $100K
evidences.content: Standard Chartered reduced its year-end 2025 Bitcoin forecast to $100,000 from $200,000, while keeping a longer-term $500,000 outlook but pushing that timeline from 2028 to 2030. The report says Bitcoin's uptrend has stalled and cites a lack of catalysts to drive it higher.
